newsSince: No material company-specific news in the June 9-23 window. Q3 FY26 earnings (May 6: revenue $808M, +90%) and the incremental multi-hundred-million CPO order (deliverable H1 CY2027) were disclosed before June 9. The only June item is a June 11 J.P. Morgan Overweight reiteration arguing CPO-adoption fears are overstated, which is sell-side opinion, not new fundamentals.

sources.label: Lumentum Q3 FY26 earnings transcript (Motley Fool)
Nvidia optics shift puts Lumentum at center of AI data centers (Yahoo)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the passives/power-bottleneck lens LITE remains peripheral (optics, not passives) and fully discovered after +115% YTD and the NVIDIA tie-up. No new evidence since June 9; June 11 JPM note is sell-side noise. Hold at rank 60.
verdicts.bull: AI networking optics/datacom leverage with NVIDIA backing.
verdicts.neutral: Strong AI exposure but off the passive/power-bottleneck core.
verdicts.bear: +115% YTD, widely owned; far from this list's bottleneck thesis.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding; not the residual alpha this page seeks.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latency lens penalizes discovery, and LITE is fully screened by investors (index adds, broad buy ratings, NVIDIA $2B). No new June 9-23 information makes the node latent again; hold at rank 90.
verdicts.bull: Clear AI datacom optics optionality; record Q4 guide.
verdicts.neutral: Good business, zero discovery premium left.
verdicts.bear: Everyone already owns the optics-AI story.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO-specific lens LITE retains the highest centrality of its four listings (NVIDIA $2B cash, purchase commitments, dedicated US fab, multi-hundred-M CPO order). But that evidence is pre-June 9; nothing new in the window (June 11 JPM reiteration only). Prior 'up' is captured at rank 41, so hold now.
verdicts.bull: Most direct NVIDIA laser/ELS/CPO candidate with cash investment, commitments and CPO order to H1 CY2027.
verdicts.neutral: Central CPO play but already heavily rerated.
verdicts.bear: Very large recent rerating; customer-concentration and capacity-ramp risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Additional CPO/ELS design wins and capacity online.
verdicts.primaryRisk: NVIDIA single-customer dependence; CPO timing slippage.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master list: scale-out bandwidth/optics content is genuine but the +115% YTD rerating is fully recognized. No new fundamental news since June 9, so hold at rank 45 with no drift.
verdicts.bull: 800G/1.6T optical links, CPO and lasers with NVIDIA backing.
verdicts.bear: Rerated optics name; scarce-bottleneck alpha mostly harvested.
